1_data_source.rule: Google Search Console Search Analytics API is the ONLY accepted source for current GSC performance data (impressions, clicks, CTR, position).

2_cached_files.rule: Cached files are allowed ONLY for historical comparison and trend analysis. They must NEVER be reported as current/live data.
2_cached_files.allowed_use: Historical comparison, trend direction, change-over-time analysis
2_cached_files.forbidden_use: Reporting as current metrics, first-click claims, scoreboard updates, Phase reports

3_manual_ui_values.rule: Manual GSC UI values provided by Qasem are USER-OBSERVED COMPARISON only. They are NOT the pipeline source of truth.
3_manual_ui_values.allowed_use: Calibrating correction factors, validating API data freshness, confirming trend direction
3_manual_ui_values.forbidden_use: Replacing API data in automated scoreboards, using as primary data source in reports

4_api_unavailability.rule: If GSC API is unavailable (auth failure, 401, 403, 429, timeout, property access denied), report GSC_API_UNAVAILABLE. STOP all metric claims. Do NOT fall back to cached/manual data.
4_api_unavailability.on_failure: Continue non-GSC tasks. Alert Qasem only if auth action is needed.

5_api_delay.rule: GSC is NOT real-time. If the API has a data delay, report the latest API-available date clearly. Do not call delayed data 'live.'

6_never_invent.rule: Never invent, estimate, or extrapolate live GSC data from any source other than the API.

7_row_limits.rule: Use rowLimit=250 minimum. If API returns exactly 250 rows, paginate with startRow until fewer than 250 rows returned.
